rules.name: playwright-headless-run
rules.evidence: Playwright (E2E tests against production) - IMPORTANT: RUN HEADLESS whenever possible

rules.name: svelte-runes-no-legacy-reactive
rules.evidence: Follow existing Svelte 5 runes patterns (no legacy reactive statements)

skill_candidates.summary: Feature development lifecycle: make changes, test locally, type-check, lint, deploy, verify with Playwright, update DEVELOPMENT.md
skill_candidates.evidence:
1. Make changes to code
2. Test locally with `npm run dev`
3. Run type checking: `npm run check`
4. Run linting: `npm run lint`
5. Deploy: `npm run deploy`
6. Run Playwright tests to verify deployment
7. Update DEVELOPMENT.md task status if applicable

always_on: Common Development Commands: npm run dev/dev:functions/build/preview usage catalog (no obligation attached)
Code Quality commands catalog: npm run check / lint / format
Testing commands catalog: npx playwright test invocation variants
Deployment commands catalog plus single-line reminder to run build+deploy 'when you are done making changes' (no specific tool call ties to being 'done')
Tech stack description: SvelteKit/TypeScript/Tailwind, Cloudflare Workers, R2/KV, Leaflet, Supabase auth
Project structure layout for frontend (src/routes, src/lib/*) and backend (functions/api/)
Key Systems - Game Flow: creation, round-based play, Haversine distance, exponential scoring, results/sharing
Key Systems - Image Management: R2 upload with EXIF GPS extraction, IMAGE_DATA KV metadata, thumbnails, visibility controls
Key Systems - User System: Supabase auth integration, USER_DATA KV profiles, stats/upload tracking, profile pictures in R2
Data Storage: IMAGE_DATA/USER_DATA/GAME_DATA KV namespace purposes
Component Patterns: use TypeScript with typed imports, consistent Tailwind styling, properly typed prop interfaces (too vague for a specific pattern)
API Patterns: proper HTTP status codes/error handling, CORS handling, multipart/form-data uploads with validation
State Management: Svelte stores for gameStore/authStore/themeStore/toastStore; auth state synced with Supabase
Testing Requirements: new features should have Playwright E2E tests; use authenticated user state for login-requiring tests; run tests after feature implementation (no single tool call to bind to)
Configuration Files list: wrangler.toml, svelte.config.js, playwright.config.ts, .cursor/rules/web-development-rules.mdc

low_confidence.reason: 'no legacy reactive statements' is a parenthetical clarifying an affirmative convention ('follow runes patterns'), not an explicit 'never/do NOT' -- could be require-read/inject instead of block

questions: Svelte 컴포넌트에서 legacy reactive statement(`$:`) 사용을 완전히 금지해서 Edit/Write를 block해야 할까요, 아니면 runes 패턴 권장 수준(require-read/inject)으로 다뤄야 할까요?
